activities.markdown:
# The 15-Minute Daily Practice

Consistency beats duration. 15 minutes every day outperforms 2 hours once a week.

**Balanced routine structure:**
| Time | Focus |
|------|-------|
| 3 min | Warm-up (scales, chromatic exercises) |
| 5 min | Technique (current challenge: barre, bends, etc.) |
| 4 min | Songs / repertoire |
| 3 min | Free play / jamming |

Always end with something that feels **good** — free play keeps you motivated.

activities.question: Why is 15 minutes daily better than 2 hours once a week?
choices: Motor skills and muscle memory build through frequent repetition, not just volume
Guitars need to rest between sessions
activities.explanation: Motor skill development (including muscle memory) requires frequent activation. Distributed practice over many days builds neural pathways far more effectively than massed practice in one session.
